parentQuestion: Damage to the optic nerve (CN II) would result in ___.

scaffolds.q: Visual information from the eye must travel to the brain to be perceived.
scaffolds.explain: The retina detects light and converts it to electrical signals, but let me show you what happens next — those signals must reach the visual cortex in the brain before you 'see' anything. The optic nerve is the highway that carries the data. Beautiful engineering.

scaffolds.q: Each eye has its own optic nerve.
scaffolds.explain: Each eye has a separate optic nerve (CN II). damage to one optic nerve affects only that eye's vision. It's a dedicated connection, one eye, one cable. Precise and vulnerable.
